title: Real-render browser style selection lab
summary: A localhost browser application compares per-buffer frontend profiles using Neovim's actual embedded UI grid and persists owner selections with exact provenance.
details: tools/nvim_ui_capture.py implements a MessagePack-RPC Neovim UI client and consumes ext_linegrid redraw events, highlight definitions, default colors, scrolling, and flush boundaries.

Each capture loads the real init.lua, parser, frontend autocmds, conceal, virtual text, line numbers, and TokyoNight/frontend highlight groups; it is not a CSS imitation or extmark-only reconstruction.
lua/custom/dans_frontend_cpp/style.lua supplies validated buffer-local overrides so alternatives never monkey-patch global renderer functions or affect real source buffers.
style_lab/catalog.json defines questions, choices, profiles, scenes, pinned source revisions, licenses, and adaptations; arbitrary choice and scene counts are supported.

tools/style_lab.py validates the catalog, generates content-addressed real-grid captures, serves static assets and JSON APIs on loopback, atomically writes selections, and optionally waits for completion.
The browser fits a slightly overflowing code panel at the largest size between 100 and 80 percent; if it still overflows at 80 percent, it restores full size and enables horizontal scrolling.
Capture width grows per scene before browser layout so long lines are never truncated by Neovim before the fit/scroll decision.

The corpus includes synthetic semantic, CUDA/Qnpeps alignment, defer, and multiline macro controls plus pinned micro-excerpts from Dear ImGui, LLVM, NVIDIA CUDA Samples, GCC, SDL, GLFW, and Khronos Vulkan Samples.

provenance.note: Owner selected a browser implementation and required actual visual alternatives with machine-readable feedback.
These files implement capture, serving, layout, persistence, and corpus metadata.

evidence.claim: Catalog provenance, profile isolation, source integrity, exact text differences, and final HTTP acknowledgement are tested.
The first real decision round-trip selected const-default with a timestamp and catalog hash.

questions: How can an agent show several frontend syntax alternatives?
Are browser examples produced by the real Neovim renderer?
Where is a style selection recorded?
How are long code lines fit or scrolled?

last_verified.method: Real server/API exercise, Chrome screenshot inspection, RGB grid inspection, selection round-trip, and isolated tests.
